title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create virtual environment and install required packages for model inference, data processing, and evaluation

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.4+ with CUDA support: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install transformers and related packages: pip install transformers accelerate sentencepiece protobuf
steps.step4: Install evaluation dependencies: pip install scikit-learn pandas numpy matplotlib seaborn scipy
steps.step5: Install API clients: pip install openai anthropic
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step7: Set random seed for reproducibility: export PYTHONHASHSEED=42

title: Download and Preprocess Source Datasets
description: Download source datasets (GSM8K, LogiQA, CommonsenseQA, MBPP) and create unified data format

steps.step1: Install datasets library: pip install datasets
steps.step2: Download GSM8K (train split): python -c "from datasets import load_dataset; load_dataset('gsm8k', 'main', split='train').save_to_disk('data/gsm8k')"
steps.step3: Download LogiQA: python -c "from datasets import load_dataset; load_dataset('lucasmccabe/logiqa', split='train').save_to_disk('data/logiqa')"
steps.step4: Download CommonsenseQA: python -c "from datasets import load_dataset; load_dataset('commonsense_qa', split='train').save_to_disk('data/csqa')"
steps.step5: Download MBPP (sanitized): python -c "from datasets import load_dataset; load_dataset('mbpp', 'sanitized', split='train').save_to_disk('data/mbpp')"
steps.step6: Create data directory structure: mkdir -p data/{raw,processed,annotations}
steps.step7: Run data statistics script to verify downloads and report dataset sizes

title: Generate Ground-Truth Chain-of-Thought Solutions
description: Use GPT-4o-mini to generate verified CoT solutions for selected problems across all domains

steps.step1: Set OpenAI API key from environment: export OPENAI_API_KEY
steps.step2: Sample problems from each domain: 400 Math, 300 Logic, 300 Commonsense, 200 Code (total: 1,200)
steps.step3: Generate CoT for Math: Use prompt 'Solve step by step. Show your reasoning clearly, numbering each step.' Output format: numbered steps
steps.step4: Generate CoT for Logic: Use prompt 'Derive the answer through logical deduction. Number each reasoning step.'
steps.step5: Generate CoT for Commonsense: Use prompt 'Explain your reasoning step by step to arrive at the answer.'
steps.step6: Generate CoT for Code: Use prompt 'Explain the solution approach step by step before providing code.'
steps.step7: Verify solutions: For math, check with Python eval; for code, run test cases; for logic/commonsense, use GPT-4o for verification
steps.step8: Filter out problems with incorrect solutions. Target: keep ~90% with correct CoT

title: Controlled Error Injection
description: Inject 6 error types at 3 positions (early/middle/late) to create corrupted CoT variants

steps.step1: Define error injection templates for each error type:
steps.step1a: Calculation: Replace correct arithmetic with off-by-one or operation errors
steps.step1b: Logic: Replace valid implication with fallacy (affirming consequent, denying antecedent)
steps.step1c: Factuality: Insert incorrect fact or definition
steps.step1d: Omission: Remove a critical intermediate step
steps.step1e: Misinterpretation: Change constraint interpretation (e.g., 'at least' to 'exactly')
steps.step1f: Premature: Add conclusion before sufficient evidence
steps.step2: For each problem, identify valid error positions: Early (step 1-2), Middle (step 3-4), Late (step 5+)
steps.step3: Create 2 corrupted variants per problem: one with error at position A, one at position B (different positions)
steps.step4: Use rule-based transformations where possible, GPT-4o-mini for complex cases
steps.step5: Ensure semantic coherence: corrupted CoT should appear plausible
steps.step6: Verify error propagation: error should lead to incorrect final answer
steps.step7: Create balanced dataset: equal distribution across error types and positions
steps.step8: Save corrupted CoTs to data/processed/corrupted_cot.jsonl with metadata: error_type, error_step, error_position_category

title: Create Train/Test Splits and Validation
description: Split data ensuring balanced distribution across domains, error types, and positions

steps.step3: Stratify by: (a) error type, (b) error position, (c) original dataset source
steps.step4: Ensure no data leakage: same base problem cannot appear in multiple splits
steps.step5: Create data statistics report: counts per domain, error type, position, split
steps.step6: Validate splits: check that distributions are balanced

title: Baseline 1: Random and Heuristic Baselines
description: Implement simple baselines for error detection, localization, and characterization

steps.step1: Random Detection Baseline: Random binary prediction (expected accuracy ~0.5, F1 depends on class balance)
steps.step2: Random Localization Baseline: Random step selection from valid range
steps.step3: Random Characterization Baseline: Uniform random selection from 6 error types
steps.step4: Majority Class Baseline: Always predict majority class from training set
steps.step5: Always-Correct Heuristic: Always predict 'no error' (measures class imbalance penalty)
steps.step6: Middle-Step Heuristic: Always predict error at middle step (tests position bias)
steps.step7: Run all baselines on validation set, save predictions to outputs/baselines/

title: Baseline 2: Simple Prompting Baseline
description: Evaluate models with basic prompting (no special introspection techniques)

steps.step2: Detection task prompt: 'Review the following reasoning. Does it contain any errors? Answer only YES or NO.'
steps.step3: Localization task prompt: 'The reasoning below contains an error. Identify the step number (1-indexed) where the first error occurs. Answer with only the step number.'
steps.step4: Characterization task prompt: 'The reasoning below has an error at step X. Classify the error type: calculation, logic, factuality, omission, misinterpretation, or premature. Answer with only the type.'
steps.step5: Run inference on all validation samples with batch_size=8 for 7B models
steps.step6: Parse outputs: extract YES/NO for detection, integers for localization, type strings for characterization
steps.step7: Compute metrics: accuracy, F1, MAE for localization, per-type F1 for characterization

title: Baseline 3: Chain-of-Thought Verification
description: Strong baseline using CoT prompting for self-verification

steps.step2: Detection prompt with CoT: 'Review the reasoning step by step. First, analyze each step for correctness. Then conclude: does the reasoning contain errors? Format: Analysis: [your step-by-step review] Answer: YES/NO'
steps.step3: Localization prompt with CoT: 'Review the reasoning. Identify where errors occur by checking each step. Then specify the first error step number. Format: Analysis: [review] Answer: [step number]'
steps.step4: Characterization prompt with CoT: 'Analyze the error at step X. What type of mistake is this? Provide reasoning then the type. Format: Analysis: [reasoning] Answer: [type]'
steps.step5: Use temperature=0.0 for deterministic outputs, max_new_tokens=512
steps.step6: Run on validation set with 3 random seeds (42, 43, 44) for ensemble stability
steps.step7: Parse structured outputs to extract final answers
steps.step8: Compute all metrics and save to outputs/baselines/cot_verification_results.json

title: Main Evaluation: Full Model Suite on IntrospectBench
description: Evaluate all target models on all three tasks with optimized prompting

steps.step1a: Local models: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (4-bit), Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B
steps.step1b: API models: GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4o (if budget allows)
steps.step3: Use best prompting strategy from baseline experiments
steps.step4: Task 1 - Detection: Binary classification with confidence scores
steps.step5: Task 2 - Localization: Step prediction with confidence
steps.step7: Run each model with 3 random seeds (42, 43, 44) by varying temperature slightly (0.0, 0.1, 0.2)
steps.step9: Track inference time per sample for efficiency analysis

title: Self vs Other Evaluation
description: Compare error detection on self-generated vs other-model-generated CoTs

steps.step1: Generate CoT solutions using each target model on the same problem set
steps.step2: For each model, create test set with: (a) self-generated CoTs, (b) other-generated CoTs (average of other 2 models)
steps.step3: Inject errors using same methodology as ground truth
steps.step4: Evaluate each model on both self and other conditions
steps.step5: Compute Self-Other Gap metric: difference in F1 scores
steps.step6: Test hypothesis H4: Models detect errors better in other models' reasoning

title: Ablation 1: Error Position Analysis
description: Study how error depth affects detection and localization accuracy

steps.step1: Group test samples by error position: Early (steps 1-2), Middle (steps 3-4), Late (step 5+)
steps.step2: For each model, compute metrics stratified by position
steps.step4: Test hypothesis H2: Localization accuracy decreases with error depth
steps.step6: Statistical test: ANOVA to compare Early vs Middle vs Late performance

title: Ablation 2: Error Type Sensitivity
description: Analyze which error types are hardest/easiest to detect

steps.step1: Group test samples by error type (6 categories)
steps.step2: Compute per-type metrics: detection F1, localization accuracy, characterization accuracy
steps.step3: Generate confusion matrix for error type classification
steps.step4: Test hypothesis: Calculation errors easier than logic errors
steps.step5: Identify error types with highest/lowest detection rates
steps.step6: Correlation analysis: error type vs domain

title: Ablation 3: Domain Transfer Analysis
description: Test cross-domain generalization of introspection capabilities

steps.step1: Train simple classifier (if applicable) or analyze zero-shot transfer
steps.step2: Condition 1: Test on same domain as training examples in prompt
steps.step3: Condition 2: Test on different domain (e.g., train examples from Math, test on Logic)
steps.step6: Test hypothesis H1: Structured domains (Math, Code) have better introspection

title: Ablation 4: Prompting Strategy Comparison
description: Compare different prompting strategies for introspection

steps.step4: Strategy C: Metacognitive prompting ('Think about your thinking')
steps.step5: Strategy D: Self-consistency (sample 5 times, majority vote)
steps.step6: Strategy E: Step-by-step reflection (explicitly verify each step)
steps.step7: Compare performance across all three tasks
steps.step8: Identify best strategy for each task type

title: Compute Novel Metrics and Statistical Tests
description: Calculate Introspection Score, PWLS, DCS and run statistical significance tests

steps.step1: Compute Introspection Score (IS) for each model: IS = (Acc_detect + Acc_localize + Acc_characterize) / 3
steps.step2: Compute Position-Weighted Localization Score with w_i = 1/sqrt(step_i)
steps.step3: Compute Domain Calibration Score: DCS = 1 - (std_domain / mean_domain)
steps.step5: Statistical significance: paired t-tests between models
steps.step6: Effect sizes: Cohen's d for meaningful differences
steps.step7: Confidence intervals: bootstrap 95% CI for all metrics
steps.step8: Correlation analysis: detection vs localization (test r > 0.7 hypothesis)

title: Test Success Criteria
description: Verify if experimental results support or refute hypotheses

steps.step1a: Check domain variation: >15% gap between best/worst domain IS scores?
steps.step1b: Check error depth effect: Monotonic decrease in localization with position?
steps.step1c: Check error type: Calculation errors detected more than logic errors?
steps.step1d: Check correlation: r > 0.7 between detection and localization?
steps.step4: Document which hypotheses are supported with statistical evidence

title: Generate Figures for Paper
description: Create all visualizations needed for the research paper

steps.step1: Figure 1: Dataset overview - domain distribution, error type distribution, error position distribution
steps.step2: Figure 2: Main results - model comparison across three tasks (bar chart with error bars)
steps.step3: Figure 3: Domain analysis - heatmap of performance by model and domain
steps.step4: Figure 4: Position analysis - line plot showing accuracy vs error depth
steps.step5: Figure 5: Error type sensitivity - grouped bar chart per error type
steps.step6: Figure 6: Self-Other Gap - paired bar chart comparing self vs other detection
steps.step7: Figure 7: Confusion matrix for error type classification
steps.step8: Figure 8: Correlation plot - detection vs localization accuracy
steps.step9: Save all figures to outputs/visualizations/ in PDF and PNG formats

title: Generate Tables for Paper
description: Create all tables needed for the research paper

steps.step1: Table 1: Dataset statistics - problems per domain, avg steps, error distribution
steps.step2: Table 2: Main results - full metrics for all models across all tasks
steps.step3: Table 3: Ablation results - position, error type, prompting comparisons
steps.step4: Table 4: Novel metrics - IS, PWLS, DCS scores per model
steps.step5: Table 5: Statistical significance - p-values for key comparisons
steps.step6: Save tables as LaTeX source files in outputs/tables/

title: Final Summary and Artifact Generation
description: Compile all results and generate final outputs

steps.step1: Generate experiment log with all commands run and timestamps
steps.step2: Create results summary: key findings, supported hypotheses, unexpected results
steps.step3: Compute total compute time and compare to budget
steps.step4: Generate dataset release package: problems, annotations, splits
steps.step5: Generate code release package: evaluation scripts, baseline implementations
